minutes_briefs.title: BC biomass energy rate change takes effect April 1, 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has ordered the BC Utilities Commission to quickly approve a new rate for the Biomass Energy Program. The Commission must issue final orders within 10 days of receiving an application from the authority. The new rate takes effect April 1, 2026. This change affects how much businesses pay for biomass energy (energy made from organic materials like wood waste).
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required, but monitor your energy bills after April 1, 2026 for the new biomass rate.

key_dates.label: BC Biomass Energy Program new rate takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 32/2026
Made under: Utilities Commission Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 13, 2026

B.C. Reg. 32/2026, deposited March 13, 2026, under theUTILITIES COMMISSION ACT[section 3]. Order in Council 75/2026, approved and ordered March 13, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that the Direction to the British Columbia Utilities Commission Respecting the Biomass Energy Program, B.C. Reg. 71/2019, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — A. DIX,Minister of Energy and Climate Solutions; S. MALCOLMSON,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Section 5 of the Direction to the British Columbia Utilities Commission Respecting the Biomass Energy Program, B.C. Reg. 71/2019, is amended (a)	by adding the following subsection: (1.1)	Within 10 days of receiving an application from the authority to do so, the commission must issue final orders so that, effective April 1, 2026, the rate set under subsection (1) is replaced as set out in Appendix 2., and (b)	in subsection (2) by striking out "subsection (1)" and substituting "subsection (1.1)". 2	The attached Appendix 2 is added.
